frameworks.name: Bayesian reinforcement learning with evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Combines reinforcement-based learning with accumulation of uncertain sensory evidence

frameworks.name: drift-diffusion / evidence accumulation
frameworks.one_line: Standard accumulation model extended to incorporate learning from outcomes

frameworks.name: standard noise-driven perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: Standard view attributing choice errors primarily to sensory noise

core_question: How does the history of recent choices and their outcomes interact with sensory evidence accumulation to shape speed-accuracy tradeoffs?
key_assumptions: Observers combine prior beliefs with sensory likelihoods via Bayesian inference
Reinforcement-based learning updates choice policy from outcomes
Sensory evidence is uncertain and must be accumulated
manipulations: Olfactory stimulus difficulty during decision task
key_findings: Rat speed and accuracy on olfactory decisions best explained by Bayesian model combining learning with evidence accumulation
Model predicted the specific pattern of trial history effects observed in behavior
interpretations: Learning is a critical factor contributing to speed-accuracy tradeoffs
Task history effects are signatures of an optimal learning strategy rather than mere biases
